session.user: Run `pytest tests/unit/plan_store/ -x -q` from /home/runner/work/erk/erk and report results.
session.assistant: I'll run the pytest command to test the plan_store module.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run pytest on plan_store tests with fail-fast and quiet mode

session.tool_result:
Exit code 1 ........................................................................ [ 44%] ...........................F =================================== FAILURES =================================== ______________________ test_add_comment_returns_string_id ______________________
backend_with_plan = (<erk_shared.plan_store.planned_pr.GitHubManagedPrBackend object at 0x7f53dfa0a8d0>, '999')
def test_add_comment_returns_string_id( backend_with_plan: tuple[ManagedPrBackend, str], ) -> None: """Backend returns comment ID as string.""" backend, pr_id = backend_with_plan
> comment_id = backend.add_comment( repo_root=Path("/repo"), plan_id=pr_id, body="Progress update: Phase 1 complete", ) E TypeError: GitHubManagedPrBackend.add_comment() got an unexpected keyword argument 'plan_id'
tests/unit/plan_store/test_plan_backend_interface.py:166: TypeError =========================== short test summary info ============================ FAILED tests/unit/plan_store/test_plan_backend_interface.py::test_add_comment_returns_string_id - TypeError: GitHubManagedPrBackend.add_comment() got an unexpected keyword argument 'plan_id' !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! stopping after 1 failures !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
session.assistant:
pytest failed: 1 test failure
**Test**: `test_add_comment_returns_string_id` **File**: `tests/unit/plan_store/test_plan_backend_interface.py:166` **Error**: `TypeError: GitHubManagedPrBackend.add_comment() got an unexpected keyword argument 'plan_id'`
**Summary**: The test is calling `backend.add_comment()` with a `plan_id` keyword argument, but the `GitHubManagedPrBackend.add_comment()` method doesn't accept that parameter name. The method signature appears to use a different parameter name (likely `pr_id` based on the recent semantic rename mentioned in the git history).
